title: Email signatures in sequences
excerpt: I have just started using the sequence feature on monday.com CRM which is great fro email campaigns etc but it doesn’t pull the email signature I have saved in monday. It makes the emails look like spam without this and …

posts.author: lilystevens (Lily)
posts.cookedHtml: I have just started using the sequence feature on monday.com CRM which is great fro email campaigns etc but it doesn’t pull the email signature I have saved in monday. It makes the emails look like spam without this and clear i have not personally emailed anyone. Would be great to have this changed

posts.author: Marcel1987 (Marcel Lang)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

Yes I have same desire. I also tried to copy a signature into the sequence editor. It didn’t transform it nicely. I tested it with a lead with my personal email address. And the siganture was deleted. Why? I tried it then with a codeblock with the signature as HTML. I copied my HTML script there. But then the app ran into an error and I can’t save anymore any sequences. So I couldn’t test. I am in contact with the support. The app itself seems not very staple. Anyway. Test the app please with signatures (images/icons). The problem is easily solvable if there is an option to use a presetted signature (“email & activities”). Thank you very much.

posts.author: jessetolppa (Jesse Tolppa)
posts.cookedHtml: Has anyone gotten any responses on this? Is the answer to only use manual email reminders in the sequences until they fix it?

posts.author: DamianFranko (Damian Franko)
posts.cookedHtml: This is something I’ve been wondering about too — having consistent, branded signatures across email sequences makes a huge difference for professionalism. In our case, we ended up using an external signature management tool that syncs with Microsoft 365 and lets us control branding across departments. I’ve heard of platforms like Exclaimer that offer that kind of centralized signature control, especially useful for larger teams. Would be great if monday.com could eventually integrate this natively into email automations. Curious to hear what others are doing as a workaround?
